title: hypserpa borneensis
intro: Hypserpa borneensis, also known as the Borneo snake vine, is a plant species native to the island of Borneo in Southeast Asia. It is a woody climber with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The plant is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antipyretic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, pain, and infection. Hypserpa borneensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, infusion, and poultice, to harness its potential health benefits.
